House Selmy of Harvest Hall
Coat of arms Three stalks of yellow wheat on brown
(Brunâtre, three stalks of wheat or)
Seat Harvest Hall
Head Lord Arstan Selmy[1]
Region Stormlands
Title Lord of Harvest Hall[1]
Overlord House Baratheon

­House Selmy of Harvest Hall is one of the principal noble houses from the Stormlands.[2] Among the greatest marcher lords, their seat is Harvest Hall in the Dornish Marches.[3][N 1] The Selmys blazon their arms with three stalks of yellow wheat on brown.[7][8]

History[ ]

Lords Selmy, Dondarrion, and Tarly represented the Dornish Marches at the Golden Wedding in 49 AC.[6]

Ser Barristan Selmy, also known as "Barristan the Bold", is one of the greatest knights of the Kingsguard. Barristan is the eldest son of Ser Lyonel Selmy.[7] He was in line to inherit the seat of House Selmy, but Barristan gave up his claim after he became a member of the Kingsguard. His cousin, who married the girl destined for Barristan, may also have taken over Barristan's claim to Harvest Hall.[9]

At a tourney at Blackhaven, a ten-year old Barristan Selmy entered the lists as a mystery knight. He was eventually unhorsed and unmasked by Prince Duncan Targaryen, earning Barristan the nickname 'Bold'.[7]

Barristan squired for Ser Manfred Swann and was knighted at sixteen years old by King Aegon V Targaryen. Barristan was named to the Kingsguard when he was twenty-three years old and had the white cloak placed on his shoulders by Ser Gerold Hightower, Lord Commander of the Kingsguard. Barristan won a tourney held at Storm's End, beating Prince Rhaegar Targaryen in the final tilt. Barristan slew Maelys the Monstrous during the War of the Ninepenny Kings.[7]

Recent Events[ ]

A Game of Thrones[ ]

Ser Barristan Selmy, Lord Commander of the Kingsguard, rides to meet King Robert I Baratheon on his return trip from the North, along with Lord Renly Baratheon and Ser Ilyn Payne.[10] Barristan is stripped of his white cloak by Robert's successor, Joffrey Baratheon, and is replaced as Lord Commander by Ser Jaime Lannister.[9] Barristan slays two gold cloaks on his way out of King's Landing.[11]

A Clash of Kings[ ]

House Selmy supports Renly during the War of the Five Kings.[12]

Posing as "Arstan Whitebeard", a squire of Strong Belwas, Ser Barristan joins the retinue of Daenerys Targaryen, judging her worth.[13]

A Storm of Swords[ ]

Arstan's identity as Barristan is revealed and he becomes one of Daenerys's most trusted advisers.[14]

A Dance with Dragons[ ]

The current head of House Selmy is Arstan Selmy, Lord of Harvest Hall.[1]

House Selmy at the end of the third century[ ]

The known Selmys during the timespan described in A Song of Ice and Fire are:

  • Lord Arstan Selmy, Lord of Harvest Hall.[1]
    • Ser Barristan Selmy, his great-uncle. Called "Barristan the Bold", a member of the Kingsguard.[1]

Historical Members[ ]

  • Lord Selmy ruling in 49 AC, a guest at the Golden Wedding.[6]
  • Unnamed Selmy, grandfather of Ser Barristan Selmy. He did not trust Dornishmen.[15]
    • Ser Lyonel Selmy, his son and the father of Barristan.[7]
  • Unnamed cousin of Barristan, married to the girl Barristan was supposed to marry before joining the Kingsguard.[9]
  • Unnamed Selmys, cousins of Barristan Selmy, with whom he played a kissing game, during his boyhood at Harvest Hall.[15]

Notes[ ]

  1. ↑ According to semi-canon sources, House Selmy is a family of landed knights.[4][5] However, the appendix of A Dance with Dragons, The World of Ice & Fire and Fire & Blood all list the Selmys as a lordly house.[1][3][6]
